title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Nancy Duane confesses to Hercule Poirot and Katchadourian that she betrayed her love, Patrick Ives. While she admits to having a relationship with him, she explains that she lied to the villagers at the King's Head Inn, claiming their relationship was pure to avoid scandal and protect Francis. Nancy expresses deep guilt over this cowardice. She asserts that Patrick and Francis actually committed suicide, despite official records and doctors claiming they died in accidents. Nancy believes Dr. Flavard, whom she considers a good man, falsified the accident reports to protect the reputations of Patrick and Francis from the stigma of suicide.

Following the confession, Poirot and Katchadourian discuss the implications of the case. They suspect Margaret Earnshaw may have attempted to prevent Katchadourian from speaking to Dr. Flavard to protect the doctor's lie. Poirot observes a shift in the nature of the murders from poison to blood and questions why Jenny Hobbs's body was moved from the Bluxton Hotel. He also notes that Thomas Brineau is the only employee who saw Richard Nigges twice.

Katchadourian struggles to identify a killer, noting that while Nancy Duane is a suspect, she has an alibi. They consider motives for Henry Nigges, who might kill for money, though his connection to the other victims remains unclear. Katchadourian suggests investigating Margaret Earnshaw and Dr. Flavard as potential suspects motivated by revenge. Finally, Katchadourian reviews a list of couples to identify an "old woman and a young man" mentioned by Raphael Burbank, but finds no matches where the woman is significantly older than the man.
